questions.text: Which of the following best describes the optimal placement of the video monitor during laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Above eye level to improve visibility over the sterile field
B) At or slightly below eye level to reduce neck strain
C) On the side opposite the surgeon for ergonomic balance
D) It must be placed directly over the patient's head

questions.explanation: Proper monitor height minimizes cervical extension and discomfort during long procedures.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about patient positioning is most accurate?

answerOptions.text: A) Positioning equipment should remain fixed and the patient should be adjusted to it
B) Patients should be placed in standard positions without modification
C) The patient should be adjusted to fit into pre-set positioning devices
D) Positioning equipment should be adapted to the patient to avoid neuromuscular injury

questions.explanation: Positioning equipment should be tailored to each patient. Forcing a patient into rigid positioning devices can cause nerve injury.

questions.text: Which of the following best represents the ideal ergonomic posture for the surgeon during laparoscopic procedures?

answerOptions.text: A) Arms abducted <30° from the trunk, elbows flexed 60–120°, wrists slightly pronated
B) Arms flexed at 90°, wrists extended, thumbs pointing downward
C) Elbows locked, arms held high, wrists in neutral position
D) Shoulders shrugged to compensate for poor table height

questions.explanation: This ergonomic posture reduces fatigue and improves fine motor control during surgery.

questions.text: A surgical assistant finds that their monitor cable isn’t long enough to allow comfortable positioning. What is the best next step?

answerOptions.text: A) Proceed with the case and move the monitor closer during surgery
B) Have the assistant switch to the surgeon’s monitor
C) Replace the cable or reposition the monitor with sufficient cable length
D) Ask the assistant to reposition themselves every 10 minutes to reduce strain

questions.explanation: Monitor placement must support proper ergonomics; correct the cable length issue before beginning the case.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about ancillary equipment setup is false?

answerOptions.text: A) All ancillary equipment should be tested for function before the procedure
B) Sterile drapes and radiation protection must be available when appropriate
C) Coagulation devices and ultrasound machines should be placed for easy access to the sterile field
D) Insufflator settings are not important as long as the pneumoperitoneum is stable

questions.explanation: Insufflator settings must remain visible to the team; pressure changes can be dangerous and must be monitored.

questions.text: During a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a surgeon wants to minimize their muscle strain and fatigue. Which strategy would be most helpful?

answerOptions.text: A) Fully extending elbows to maximize instrument reach
B) Keeping arms close to the trunk with thumbs pointed down
C) Standing on a step stool to align body with the monitor
D) Rotating positions with the assistant every 20 minutes

questions.explanation: Step stools help correct height mismatches and allow proper ergonomic alignment.

questions.text: Which best describes proper setup for intraoperative imaging during laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Ensure table can tilt side-to-side for C-arm clearance
B) Position the table so the C-arm can fit over the surgical drapes
C) Ensure the table allows the C-arm or X-ray plate to pass under the table or patient
D) Place the C-arm over the sterile instrument table for optimal access

questions.explanation: For procedures requiring imaging, the operating table must allow the C-arm or X-ray plate to pass beneath the patient.
